critique.summary: The article presents a browser-based interactive simulator for the three-body problem, showcasing its features and functionality. It highlights the challenges of simulating complex gravitational interactions in a visually engaging manner.
mainPoints: The simulator allows users to interact with a 3D representation of the three-body problem.
It demonstrates the complexities of gravitational interactions among three celestial bodies.
critique.truthfulness: The claims about the simulator's functionality appear to be accurate based on the description provided. However, without additional sources or user feedback, the overall reliability of the claims cannot be fully assessed.
considerations: The article does not provide any user reviews or testimonials about the simulator's effectiveness.
There is no mention of the underlying algorithms or physics used in the simulation.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers on the interactive 3D three-body problem simulator, with users providing feedback on UI issues, solver accuracy, and related projects. Some comments highlight technical aspects like solver methods, while others share similar projects or express enthusiasm.

comments.author: hazrmard
comments.text: Very cool! Interesting how the choice of solver affects the solution. Euler doesn't handle misbehaved equations very well. You can see this in the Helix setup where the bodies just fly off.
comments.analysis: This comment accurately points out the impact of numerical solvers on simulation stability, specifically Euler's limitations, which is a well-known issue in computational physics.

comments.author: westurner
comments.text: Notes re: Multi-body problems on "Show HN: Browser-based interactive 3D Three-Body problem simulator" (2025) https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=45981336 which has Velocity Verlet integration method, 4th-order Runge-Kutta (RK4), and Kepler solvers
comments.analysis: Provides useful context about different numerical methods used in similar simulators, indicating familiarity with the topic and adding value to the discussion.

comments.author: ubezon
comments.text: Very cool! nice to be able to export json too
comments.analysis: Expresses positive feedback and highlights a useful feature (JSON export), which is likely accurate and constructive for users interested in data handling.

comments.author: neondude
comments.text: Feedback : The ui covers more than half my screen on mobile browser
comments.analysis: Provides practical user interface feedback that is plausible and useful for improving mobile usability.

comments.author: hermitcrab
comments.text: Random3D seems to always have all the stars heading off to infinity.
comments.analysis: Observes a consistent behavior in the simulation which may indicate a bug or limitation in the random initial conditions or solver.

comments.author: koolala
comments.text: When I hit Random 3d to see a normal three-body they yeet out of existence far far away.
comments.analysis: Similar to other comments, this user notes that the simulation bodies escape to infinity, likely reflecting solver or initial condition issues.

comments.author: modeless
comments.text: How many of these are there? https://trisolarchaos.com/
comments.analysis: Asks a relevant question and shares a related resource, contributing to the broader context of three-body problem simulators.

comments.author: kurthong
comments.text: https://solgrbit.d9ng.co.kr/ Had a similar idea and built a prototype for it.
comments.analysis: Shares a related project, which adds value by showing parallel work and potential alternatives.

comments.author: curseofcasandra
comments.text: The tri-Solarians could have just used this!
comments.analysis: A humorous, off-topic comment referencing science fiction, adding little to the technical discussion.

comments.author: kordlessagain
comments.analysis: A brief, unclear comment lacking context or explanation, providing minimal value to the conversation.

comments.author: kurthong
comments.text: Had a similar idea and built a prototype for it. :)
comments.analysis: Expresses enthusiasm and mentions a related project, but without additional details, it is less informative.
